director_monologue: The Genesis Directive is acknowledged. We are calibrating our formalization engines on the legendary IMO 1988 Problem 6 using Vieta Jumping. The Critic has provided a valid starting vector with no deception detected yet. We will establish the foundational Lean 4 definitions. Alpha will define the algebraic relation and the non-square assumption. Beta will construct the minimal counterexample framework using the well-ordering of the natural numbers, setting the trap for the infinite descent.
alpha_directive: Define the core predicate in Lean 4: `def is_valid_pair (a b k : \mathbb{N}) : Prop := a^2 + b^2 = k * (a * b + 1)`. Next, define the non-square property: `def not_perfect_square (k : \mathbb{N}) : Prop := \forall m : \mathbb{N}, m^2 \neq k`. Finally, bundle these into a structure or set representing the counterexample space for a fixed k.
beta_directive: Establish the well-ordering foundation for the infinite descent. Formulate the existence of a minimal pair: `lemma exists_minimal_counterexample (k : \mathbb{N}) (h : not_perfect_square k) (h_exists : \exists a b : \mathbb{N}, is_valid_pair a b k) : \exists A B : \mathbb{N}, is_valid_pair A B k \wedge (\forall a b : \mathbb{N}, is_valid_pair a b k \rightarrow A + B \leq a + b)`. Prepare the exact structural induction over the sum A + B.
